overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a total follower count of 3580. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn showing higher engagement on specific posts. Content focuses on research, job opportunities, and conference presentations related to computational social science and disinformation.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research findings, publications, and ongoing projects in computational social science.
Content focusing on the study and detection of disinformation and coordinated inauthentic behavior.
Posts advertising job openings, postdoc positions, and research opportunities for students and researchers.
Announcements and discussions related to conference presentations and methodological approaches.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach and engagement.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to improve visibility.
Engage more actively with comments and replies on LinkedIn and Bluesky.
Use platform-specific hashtags to improve discoverability (e.g., #AcademicTwitter on Mastodon, relevant industry hashtags on LinkedIn).
Consider using visual content (images, videos) to enhance engagement.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of research announcements and community engagement (PhD opportunities). Content performs well in terms of reposts, indicating strong resonance with the audience.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has the lowest follower count and engagement compared to the other platforms. Posting frequency is also lower. Focus on sharing thought-provoking content and engaging in relevant discussions to build a stronger following.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn posts receive the highest engagement in terms of likes and comments, particularly job postings and grant announcements. This suggests the LinkedIn audience is highly interested in career-related and research funding information.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
